channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Indian Funds Pour ₹80,000 Cr Into Stocks Amid Global Crisis, FII Sell-off
item.description: Indian mutual funds sharply cut cash holdings in March 2026, investing an estimated ₹80,000 crore into equities as markets corrected amid rising global tensions. This move contrasts with record foreign portfolio investor (FPI) outflows of ₹1.17 lakh crore for the month. While benchmark indices like the Nifty 50 fell over 11%, fund managers viewed the downturn as an opportunity, showing confidence in India's economy despite higher oil prices and currency pressures. Domestic institutional investors helped offset foreign selling, but overall market sentiment remained cautious.

item.title: India Mutual Fund Assets Slowest in 3 Years; SIPs Bolster Market
item.description: Indian mutual fund assets grew a muted 12.2% to ₹73.73 lakh crore in FY26, the slowest pace in three years. This moderation occurred despite a robust 20.7% rise in SIP contributions to ₹3.5 lakh crore, signaling sustained investor discipline. Volatility, geopolitical tensions, and FII outflows marked the year, though valuations have since improved, positioning India as a relative value play.

item.title: India IPOs Launch: Citius Transnet, Mehul Telecom Amid Caution
item.description: India's primary market is gearing up for new offerings this week, led by Citius Transnet Investment Trust's ₹1,105 crore infrastructure IPO and Mehul Telecom's ₹29 crore SME issue. These launches occur as a challenging IPO environment persists, with many recent listings trading below their issue price in a 'discount trap' reflecting investor caution.

item.title: M&M, Waaree Energies: Analysts Spot Buy Signals Amid Divergent Sectors
item.description: Mahindra & Mahindra (M&M) and Waaree Energies are attracting investor attention following robust technical breakouts. Angel One's Osho Krishan issued BUY recommendations for both, citing bullish patterns and indicator alignment. M&M’s recovery above its 20-day EMA, supported by a bullish gap, suggests sustained upward momentum. Waaree Energies has consolidated above its 200-day SMA, forming bullish chart patterns like a double bottom and flag breakout. Both stocks are positioned for near to medium-term accumulation based on these technical setups.

item.title: Tata Capital Lock-In Expiry: 67% Shares Unlocked, Liquidity Pressure Builds
item.description: Tata Capital's lock-in period expires on April 13, 2026, making approximately 67% of its equity, valued at nearly ₹93,742.4 crore, available for trading. While analyst sentiment remains overwhelmingly positive with all seven tracking analysts issuing 'Buy' recommendations and price targets reaching ₹410, the sheer volume of unlocked shares introduces significant liquidity pressure. The company's stock has traded near its IPO price of ₹326, showing modest gains, setting the stage for potential volatility as large shareholders decide on their next moves.

item.title: Oil Spike on Geopolitics; India Markets Face Volatility, Q4 Earnings Key
item.description: Indian markets are set for a volatile trading session after escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and a sharp rise in crude oil prices. The GIFT Nifty futures point to a significant gap-down opening. As global instability grows, investor attention will also turn to domestic Q4 earnings from ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company and Swaraj Engines, which could reveal underlying strengths or weaknesses despite the broader concerns.

item.title: GIFT Nifty Flags Weak Open as Brent Crude Nears $104
item.description: Indian stock markets are poised for a subdued opening as GIFT Nifty trades lower at 23,784. This follows Friday's rally where Nifty 50 and Sensex gained over 1.2%. Meanwhile, Brent crude oil prices surged, nearing $104 a barrel, adding inflationary concerns.

item.title: FIIs Boost Index Futures, Fueling Auto and FMCG Rally
item.description: Foreign Institutional Investors (FIIs) are showing a significant bullish trend in Indian equity index futures, boosting market sentiment. This FII shift is benefiting the Auto and Fast-Moving Consumer Goods (FMCG) sectors, which display strong technicals and economic support. The Auto sector benefits from stability and growth, while FMCG shows resilience despite rising costs. Analysts see potential upside for both.

item.title: AI in India: Boosts Literacy, Hurts Traders as SEBI Acts
item.description: AI is changing India's finance sector. While it promises to boost financial literacy, its use in trading often leads to losses for retail investors. SEBI is introducing new rules for AI trading and financial influencers to prevent market manipulation and protect investors. The real value of AI might be in education and inclusion, not just faster trading.

item.title: India's Defense Boom: Specialized Firms Tap Global Demand
item.description: India's defense sector is experiencing a broad spending increase beyond public sector giants. Key players like Astra Microwave Products, Solar Industries, and Zen Technologies are benefiting from major defense approvals for missiles, artillery, drones, and electronics. These firms are evolving from component suppliers to strategic partners, capitalizing on domestic needs and global shortages to drive multi-year growth through domestic production and exports.

item.title: Kalyan Jewellers Sales Surge 64% But Gold Prices Pose Risks
item.description: Kalyan Jewellers India announced a 64% year-on-year revenue surge for Q4 FY26, fueled by strong domestic sales and expansion. While analysts like Motilal Oswal maintain a 'Buy' rating, the company faces challenges from high gold prices, geopolitical issues, and recent stock underperformance, despite short-term gains.

item.title: IKS Health to Buy TruBridge for $600M in Bid for RCM Growth
item.description: Inventurus Knowledge Solutions (IKS) is nearing a deal to buy Nasdaq-listed TruBridge for about $600 million. This acquisition would be IKS's largest and boost its position in healthcare revenue cycle management (RCM). TruBridge is working to improve its finances after missed earnings and audit adjustments, while IKS uses AI for efficiency.

item.title: War, AI Push Legal Chiefs into Boardroom Strategy
item.description: Global conflicts and AI are forcing companies to rethink strategy, making legal advice essential for board decisions. Businesses face higher risks in supply chains and with partners. Top legal expertise is now key to navigating today's uncertain business world.
